frameworks.name: rhythmic content representation
frameworks.one_line: visual brain rhythms carry stimulus content, not only modulate gain

frameworks.name: multivariate decoding of oscillatory signals
frameworks.one_line: frequency-resolved decoding disentangles feedforward and feedback content

frameworks.name: alpha-band feedback propagation
frameworks.one_line: alpha dynamics support content-specific top-down signaling in visual cortex

frameworks.name: predictive / generative vision (perception, imagery, prediction)
frameworks.one_line: framework where internally generated content shapes visual processing

core_question: Do rhythmic neural signals in the visual system represent and communicate specific visual content, beyond modulating responses?
key_assumptions: neural oscillations carry decodable content, not just gain
different frequency bands index different directional pathways
feedforward and feedback content can be separated by band
manipulations: comparison across visual tasks: perception, imagery, prediction
contrast of feedforward vs feedback signal directions
key_findings: rhythmic signals carry decodable content during perception, imagery, and prediction
frequency bands distinguish feedforward from feedback content transfer
alpha-band activity preferentially carries content-specific feedback signals
interpretations: visual rhythms function as a channel for content representation and routing
alpha dynamics are central to top-down content propagation in vision
